regimeSub: Trump tears up Iran ceasefire, Spain tariffs add geopolitical noise but Brent holds at $78 as OPEC+ supply absorbs shock.
themes: Trump Jul 9 rips up Iran ceasefire & orders Spain trade cutoff → EuroStoxx dropped 1.6% worst session in 3 months but VIX at 15.03 shows no contagion to US risk assets
BOK Jul 9 signals need to hike on higher inflation and growth → hawkish tilt confirms global CB hawkish synchronization with Fed/BOJ; USDKRW steady at 1500
Retail sales Jul 12 beat forecasts despite Iran war impact → consumer resilience sustains GDP 2.1% narrative but CPI 4.2%/core PCE 3.4% keeps Warsh's hike threat alive
Citi hikes TSMC price target 30%+ ahead of earnings → HBM momentum continues with SK Hynix surpassing Samsung in market cap; KOSPI at 7475 outperforms SPX YTD

signalNotes.name: US Real Rate
signalNotes.note: Policy rate 3.63% vs CPI 4.2% = -57bp real rate; still accommodative despite Warsh hawkish rhetoric on 2% target

signalNotes.name: 2s10s Curve
signalNotes.note: 2s at 4.16%, 10s at 4.54% = +38bp steepener; term premium building on fiscal/inflation concerns

signalNotes.name: HY Credit Spread
signalNotes.note: VIX 15.03 implies HY spreads contained; risk-on despite Trump Iran/Spain escalation

signalNotes.name: VIX
signalNotes.note: VIX 15.03 remarkably sanguine given Jul 9 geopolitical shock; equity vol sellers in control

signalNotes.name: Brent Crude
signalNotes.note: Brent $78.51 stable at pre-war levels; OPEC+ Jul 6 expansion offsetting Iran ceasefire collapse

signalNotes.name: Gold
signalNotes.note: Gold $4093 off $4133 highs; worst monthly drop since 2008 per Jul 1 as DXY stabilizes at 101

signalNotes.name: USD / KRW
signalNotes.note: USDKRW 1500 flat; BOK hawkish Jul 9 signal anchors won despite Fed hike optionality

signalNotes.name: Semiconductor SOX
signalNotes.note: Citi +30% TSMC PT, Nvidia Q1 85% rev growth; HBM leadership shift to SK Hynix sustains Korea semis bid

headline: Trump's Iran ceasefire collapse and Spain tariffs inject geopolitical noise but OPEC+ supply keeps Brent anchored near $78 while Warsh's Fed maintains September hike optionality on sticky 4.2% CPI.
